content.question: Is the political reversibility of tracking reforms (evident in Poland 2016) a feature of the reform design, stakeholder communication, or deeper resistance to detracking from elite-school constituencies? Would this risk apply to Hungary?
content.reduction_path: Qualitative study of Polish reform actors (policymakers, school leaders, parents) and reversals to identify the specific coalitions and framings that drove reversal; parallel stakeholder interviews in Hungary to assess whether gimnázium prestige and historical legitimacy present similar reversal risk
